skill.name: docx
skill.description: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction for .docx files.
skill.summary: Full-featured DOCX skill for creating, editing, and analyzing Word documents with tracked changes support.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 1082 potential issues across 59 files, but most are false positives. External command findings (235) are documentation examples showing bash commands for users to run, not executable code. Network URL findings (426) are XML namespace declarations in official ECMA/ISO XSD schema files, not actual network requests. Legitimate filesystem access is required for document processing. Some weak crypto warnings are false positives from XML schema text patterns. One concern: the skill requires external dependencies (pandoc, LibreOffice, docx npm package) that execute shell commands - users should verify these are installed from trusted sources.

high_findings.title: External dependency installation required
high_findings.description: The skill requires installing external tools via system package manager (sudo apt-get install). While the SKILL.md documentation shows these commands for users to run, this creates potential supply chain risk if users blindly install unverified packages.

medium_findings.title: Zip slip vulnerability in unpack script
medium_findings.description: The unpack.py script uses zipfile.extractall() without path validation, potentially allowing zip slip attacks if processing malicious .docx files.

medium_findings.title: Broad filesystem access for document processing
medium_findings.description: The skill reads and writes arbitrary files on the filesystem. This is legitimate for document processing but requires user trust.

low_findings.title: XML namespace URLs in schema files
low_findings.description: Hundreds of XSD schema files contain w3.org and ecma-international.org URLs. These are XML namespace declarations required by the OOXML specification, not network requests. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Cryptographic algorithm warnings in schema files
low_findings.description: XSD files contain text patterns like 'sha' and 'md' in XML schema type definitions. These are not actual cryptographic operations. Dismissed as false positive.

content.user_title: Create and edit Word documents with tracked changes
content.value_statement: Working with Word documents programmatically is complex. This skill provides comprehensive DOCX creation, editing, and analysis capabilities with full support for tracked changes, comments, and formatting preservation.
actual_capabilities: Create new Word documents from scratch using JavaScript/TypeScript with the docx library
Edit existing documents with full OOXML access for complex formatting changes
Implement tracked changes (redlines) showing insertions and deletions professionally
Extract text and comments while preserving document structure
Convert documents to markdown for review and to images for visual analysis
Handle document metadata, sections, headers, footers, and embedded media
limitations: Requires external dependencies: pandoc, LibreOffice, poppler-utils, and the docx npm package
Complex OOXML edits require understanding XML structure and namespace handling
Tracked changes implementation requires careful RSID management for professional results
Cannot execute code - all operations require user to run scripts locally

use_cases.title: Legal contract review
use_cases.description: Create redlined versions of contracts showing all proposed changes with proper tracked changes formatting that legal professionals expect.

use_cases.title: Academic paper collaboration
use_cases.description: Edit research papers with tracked changes and comments, allowing co-authors to review and accept/reject modifications.

use_cases.title: Business document automation
use_cases.description: Generate standardized business documents (proposals, reports, letters) from templates with dynamic content insertion.

prompt_templates.title: Simple text extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text content from this Word document and summarize the key points: [attach document.docx]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick document analysis without formatting concerns

prompt_templates.title: Create document from outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional Word document with the following structure: [provide outline]. Include proper headings, numbered sections, and a table of contents.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating new documents from structured content

prompt_templates.title: Redline contract changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this contract and create a redlined version with the following changes: [list changes]. Use tracked changes so the other party can see what was modified.
prompt_templates.scenario: Legal document review requiring professional redlines

prompt_templates.title: Batch document processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process these 10 Word documents and extract specific data fields (client name, date, amount) from each into a summary table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Automated data extraction from multiple documents

output_examples.output: Document summary provided with key clauses identified, followed by full extracted text organized by section
Professional .docx file with title page, executive summary, scope of work, timeline, budget sections, and proper formatting

output_examples.input: Add tracked changes to revise payment terms
output_examples.output: Redlined document showing deleted '30 days' and inserted '60 days' with proper RSID tracking for professional review

best_practices: Always read the full documentation files (docx-js.md, ooxml.md) before starting complex operations
Use the redlining workflow for legal, academic, or business documents where change tracking is essential
Verify XML changes with grep before running scripts, and validate final documents with pandoc conversion
Batch related changes together (3-10 per script) for easier debugging and incremental progress
anti_patterns: Do not replace entire sentences when only changing a few words - only mark the specific text that changes
Avoid using markdown line numbers for XML edits - they do not map to document structure
Never skip RSID assignment for tracked changes - always use consistent RSIDs from the unpack script
Do not run pack.py --force without validating XML first - the resulting document may be corrupt

faq.question: What dependencies do I need to install?
faq.answer: Required: pandoc (text extraction), docx npm package (creating documents), LibreOffice (PDF conversion), poppler-utils (image conversion), and defusedxml (secure XML parsing). Install with: sudo apt-get install pandoc libreoffice poppler-utils && npm install -g docx && pip install defusedxml

faq.question: Can this skill edit documents created in Microsoft Word?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with any standard .docx file including those created in Microsoft Word, Google Docs (exported), LibreOffice Writer, and other word processors that use the OOXML format.

faq.question: How do tracked changes work?
faq.answer: Tracked changes use w:ins (insertion) and w:del (deletion) XML elements with RSID identifiers. The skill implements these at the XML level, producing professional redlines that Word recognizes and displays properly.

faq.question: Can I convert tracked changes to final text?
faq.answer: Yes, use pandoc with --track-changes=accept to accept all changes, --track-changes=reject to reject all, or --track-changes=all to preserve them in the output.

faq.question: What if my document has comments?
faq.answer: Comments are stored in word/comments.xml and referenced from document.xml. Use raw XML access to read or modify comments, or convert to markdown with pandoc to see them in text form.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use on sensitive documents?
faq.answer: The skill runs entirely locally - no data is sent to external servers. However, it requires filesystem access and executes local scripts. Review the code and only use on documents you trust the AI to process.
